note: CR-1..CR-8 conceptual fixtures (Decision 0009 / R3 amendment). Deterministic consistency artifacts: they exercise the concrete/sound-reason typing over the existing verdict registry. Statuses use semantic IDs; strictly_sound is DERIVED, never a registry verdict. No empirical claim.

fixtures.title: concrete and sound reasoning
fixtures.setup: true premises / sound direct knowledge; adequate governing standards; correctly bound metaorthemmata; faithful execution; correct truth-linked placement

fixtures.title: concrete and unsound reasoning (defective inherited framework)
fixtures.setup: historically accepted but defective premise framework (defective governing configuration); faithful execution; wrong result; no material case-specific binding is at issue (zero-burden rule: GOV_TOKEN_ADEQUATE not in the required path)

fixtures.title: adequate pathway, rare miss
fixtures.setup: sufficiently reliable reasoning procedure; proper binding; faithful execution; rare incorrect result; ex-ante justified

fixtures.title: correct result through corrupt concrete rationality
fixtures.setup: defective culturally inherited governing standard; result correct by compensating error; truth linkage fails; no material case-specific binding is at issue (zero-burden rule: GOV_TOKEN_ADEQUATE not in the required path)

fixtures.title: mental/external category error with faithful execution
fixtures.setup: actor treats mental conceivability as sufficient for external possibility; argument proceeds validly from the faulty bridge; premise/bridge standard (policy level) fails; no material case-specific binding is at issue (zero-burden rule: GOV_TOKEN_ADEQUATE not in the required path)

fixtures.title: false tawatur: dependent sources bound as independent
fixtures.setup: tawatur-like metaortheme adequate; metaorthemma binds a source set sharing one upstream dependency as independent (binding failure, failing conjunct ScopeCorrect); execution faithful; epistemic-status placement overconfident

fixtures.title: evaluator symmetry: the auditor's audit is itself audited
fixtures.setup: reviewer diagnoses another actor's reasoning as corrupted; the reviewer's own source selection and binding are reified as a second episode and scored under the same verdict vector; no privileged evaluator exemption

audit_of_audit.note: the auditor's own episode record; same registry, same aggregation

fixtures.title: negative control: zero-burden rule
fixtures.setup: fixed rule with no material case-specific binding; no metaorthemma exists; GOV_TOKEN_ADEQUATE not in the required path (M1 zero-burden rule); type and execution suffice
